Course participants have the option to attend class synchronously via Zoom or review class recordings on their own time

tools.name: How to Write and Submit Federal Grant Applications
tools.description: It is particularly designed for individuals and organizations who are not affiliated with a higher education institution and/or do not have previous experience applying for a federal grant. This workshop includes practical advice to help you craft your grant application. The presenters also share their insights into the complex process of submitting an application through the Grants.gov portal

tools.name: The Recovery Hub for American Women Writers

tools.name: eLaboratories

Through our courses, forums, events, and more, practitioners can share their passions, grow their expertise, and shape the discourse of a vibrant, innovative, and inclusive community

tools.description: Lib-Static is a provocation to rethink how we do digital infrastructure in libraries to recenter our technology choices around sustainable, pragmatic, and minimal approaches.

tools.name: DocuScope
tools.description: Making the invisible visible through natural language processing and interactive visualization

tools.name: FairCopy
tools.description: FairCopy is a simple and powerful tool for reading, transcribing, and encoding text.

tools.name: Metavus
tools.description: Metavus is about collecting, describing, and disseminating resources online, putting the power and control in your hands, to help you build and maintain digital collections that meet your needs.

tools.name: eScriptorium
tools.description: eScriptorium: A Digital Text Production Pipeline for Print and Handwritten Texts using machine learning techniques.

tools.name: Leo
tools.description: Store, organize, and transcribe your documents with the power of AI

tools.name: Canopy IIIF
tools.description: An open-source static site generator designed for fast creation, contextualization, and customization of a discovery-focused digital scholarship and collections website using IIIF APIs.

tools.name: AllMaps
tools.description: Curating, georeferencing and exploring for IIIF maps. Millions of maps are available through IIIF, across libraries, archives and museums worldwide. Allmaps makes it easier and more inspiring to curate, georeference and explore collections of digitized maps.

tools.name: Mapwarper
tools.description: It's a free to use, open source map warper / map georectifier, and image georeferencer tool for individuals and small groups.

tools.name: KoboToolBox
tools.description: Intuitive and adaptable data tools to maximize your impact. A data collection, management, and visualization platform used globally to accelerate positive social change.

tools.name: Cinemetrics
tools.description: Cinemetrics tools not only let one record data to analyze movies as Yuri Tsivian did in his Intolerance study, but also publishes the gathered data on this web site for everyone to access.

tools.name: ELAN
tools.description: ELAN is an annotation tool for audio and video recordings.

tools.name: Export Comments
tools.description: Export Social Media Comments

tools.name: LEAF-VRE
tools.description: The Linked Editing Academic Framework - A Virtual Research Environment for the Humanities

tools.name: kraken
tools.description: kraken is a turn-key OCR system optimized for historical and non-Latin script material.

tools.name: Open Funding
tools.description: A guide for researching ways of funding open source projects.
